common_name: White Queen Caladium (Classic Large White-Veined Fancy Caladium)

planting.note: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost date when soil has warmed to at least 65°F; plant 1–2 inches deep in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic (pH 5.5–6.5) soil in partial to full shade; White Queen is one of the finest white caladiums for brightening deep shade areas.

directions: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial to full shade; White Queen is one of the most classic and widely grown fancy-leaved caladium cultivars — producing large, heart-shaped leaves with pure-white centers, green margins, and distinctive red veins radiating from the central midrib through the white tissue, creating a dramatic three-color leaf pattern. The large white leaf areas reflect light effectively in shaded positions, making White Queen one of the brightest and most illuminating shade plants available — a single established clump can dramatically lighten a dark corner. Caladiums are among the premier foliage plants for Gulf South shade gardens, thriving in the hot, humid conditions that challenge many other shade plants. They emerge from tubers in late spring, develop through summer, and go dormant with the first cool fall temperatures (typically October in zone 9). In zone 9, the tubers can be left in the ground over winter if protected with 4 inches of mulch, or lifted and stored in a dry, warm location. For best performance, provide consistent moisture and avoid direct afternoon sun, which bleaches the white foliage and scorches the leaf margins. Feed monthly with a balanced fertilizer during the active growing season. The combination of heat tolerance, low light requirement, and spectacular foliage makes caladiums essential for Gulf South shade garden design.

common_name: Red Flash Caladium (Red-Centered Green-Margined Fancy Caladium)

planting.note: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost date when soil has warmed to at least 65°F; plant 1–2 inches deep in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial to full shade; Red Flash is one of the most sun-tolerant caladium cultivars, performing acceptably in 3–4 hours of morning sun.

directions: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial shade to partial sun; Red Flash is one of the most striking and popular fancy-leaved caladium cultivars — producing large, heart-shaped leaves with vivid red centers, green margins, and distinctive pink to white spots scattered across the red central area, creating a complex, colorful pattern unique among caladium cultivars. The red center, spotted texture, and dark-green margins combine into a leaf with more visual complexity and depth than solid-colored cultivars. Red Flash is notable among caladiums for its higher sun tolerance — it performs acceptably in morning sun positions (3–4 hours direct sun) that would bleach or damage most white-leaved caladiums. This sun tolerance makes Red Flash more versatile for partially shaded locations that receive some direct morning light. The large, boldly patterned leaves create outstanding tropical texture in mixed shade plantings, container gardens, and as mass ground covers under trees. In zone 9, Red Flash produces foliage from late April or May through October before cold temperatures trigger dormancy. The tubers increase in size each year, producing progressively larger and more numerous leaves as the clumps mature. Red Flash is also well-suited to container production, where the combination of good sun tolerance and striking foliage makes it an outstanding patio and deck plant.

common_name: Florida Cardinal Caladium (Vivid Red All-Red Caladium)

planting.note: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost date when soil has warmed to at least 65°F; plant 1–2 inches deep in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial shade; Florida Cardinal was developed at the University of Florida and is one of the finest red caladiums for heat and humidity tolerance.

directions: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial shade; Florida Cardinal is a University of Florida breeding introduction — a vivid red caladium with predominantly red leaf coloration (deep rose-red to cardinal-red center) transitioning to narrow green margins, creating a boldly red-dominated leaf effect that is one of the most vivid pure-red caladiums available. The nearly solid red leaf, with red dominant over most of the surface and only a narrow green border at the margin, creates a strong, high-contrast visual impact in shade garden plantings where the vivid red stands out dramatically against the typical green-dominated background. Florida Cardinal was selected by University of Florida breeders for superior heat and humidity tolerance — attributes particularly relevant for Gulf South gardeners who grow caladiums through the intense summer conditions of zone 9. Caladiums are among the few ornamentals that actually perform better in heat and high humidity than in cool conditions — the tropical conditions of the Gulf South summer suit them ideally. In zone 9, Florida Cardinal grows from late April through October with essentially no special care beyond moisture and shade. Mass plantings of Florida Cardinal under trees or in shaded foundation beds create a carpet of vivid red that transforms the appearance of the shade garden from late spring through fall. The tubers increase each year and can be divided in spring to multiply planting material.

common_name: Miss Muffet Caladium (Dwarf Chartreuse-Speckled Compact Caladium)

planting.note: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost date when soil has warmed to at least 65°F; plant 1–2 inches deep in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial shade; Miss Muffet is significantly more compact than standard fancy-leaved caladiums — use it at the front of borders, in containers, and as edging where smaller caladiums are more appropriate.

directions: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial to full shade; Miss Muffet is one of the most distinctive dwarf caladium cultivars — producing small (4–6 inch), light chartreuse-green to lime-yellow leaves heavily speckled with small red to pink spots that are scattered across the leaf surface in an irregular, freckled pattern. The unique combination of chartreuse-yellow-green leaf color and contrasting red spotting creates an unusual and eye-catching foliage effect quite different from the large white, red, or pink solid-colored standard fancy caladiums. The chartreuse-yellow tone is particularly effective in creating bright spots in shade — the warm yellow-green color reflects light more than darker greens, providing a light-reflective effect in partial shade similar to but visually distinct from the pure-white of 'White Queen'. Miss Muffet's compact size (8–14 inches tall and wide) makes it appropriate for container gardening, edging uses, and front-of-border plantings where the standard 18–24 inch caladiums would be out of scale. The dwarf strap-leaved habit (some leaves are partially strap-shaped rather than fully heart-shaped) is consistent with other compact caladium forms. Pair with White Queen or Red Flash for a visually interesting mixed caladium planting with complementary color combinations.

common_name: Thai Beauty Caladium (Pink and White Variegated Fancy Caladium)

planting.note: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost date when soil has warmed to at least 65°F; plant 1–2 inches deep in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial to full shade; Thai Beauty is a larger, more vigorous caladium than most fancy-leaved cultivars — provide ample space and consistent moisture for maximum leaf development.

directions: Plant tubers in spring after the last frost in moist, well-drained, humus-rich, acidic soil in partial to full shade; Thai Beauty is one of the most strikingly patterned and vigorous fancy-leaved caladiums available — producing large to extra-large heart-shaped leaves with a complex pattern of pink and white variegation on a green base, with irregular pink and white sectors, streaks, and markings creating a kaleidoscopic pattern that varies from leaf to leaf. The intricate, non-repeating pattern of each leaf — with pink, white, and green sectors arranged differently on each successive leaf — gives the plant an unusual, almost watercolor-painted appearance that is visually complex and continuously interesting as new leaves unfurl with slightly different patterning. Thai Beauty grows vigorously and produces large leaves, creating an impressive tropical foliage mass in summer shade gardens. The soft pink and white tones create a cooler, more pastel effect than the bold red-and-white contrast of 'White Queen' or the pure color masses of 'Florida Cardinal' — making Thai Beauty an excellent choice for mixed caladium plantings where different color combinations are desired. As with all fancy-leaved caladiums, provide consistent moisture throughout the growing season, mulch heavily to maintain soil moisture and moderate root temperature, and allow the foliage to yellow naturally in fall before removing the withered stems.
